commentary_text: The model names Australia the slight favorite at 35%, while sportsbooks heavily back USA at 60%, a 26-percentage-point gap on the USA win. The draw probability also diverges sharply, with the model at 32% versus the market at 22%. These gaps could reflect sportsbook access to recent lineup news, injury updates, or form information unavailable to the model's last training cycle. Alternatively, the market may be anchoring on pre-tournament expectations or narrative strength around one team. The dashboard flags this structural disagreement—different favorites, compressed draw odds—as a signal worth monitoring rather than a resolution.
